title: Groq free tier TPM cap blocks large-corpus cathedral runs
body: The Groq free tier enforces 6,000 tokens-per-minute for llama-3.3-70b-versatile. The Cathedral Effect corpus substrate is ~11,743 input tokens per call — nearly 2× the per-minute budget. Even a 120-second inter-call delay cannot prevent 429 Rate Limit errors once the TPM window is saturated during retry backoff. Fix: use paid tier, truncate corpus to <5K tokens, or switch to a vendor with a more generous free quota (Together AI, Fireworks AI). This is a vendor tier issue, not a Cathedral Effect failure.
author: Knight/Cursor

title: 70B cold baseline sealed: 0% HOT, 16% HIT on Cranewell bank
body: Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) cold condition: n=50 questions, HOT=0%, HIT=16%, MISS=84%. Average latency 1.4s, cost $0.00014/call. Larger model adds ~6 percentage points of HIT vs the 8B baseline (~10% HIT in K511) — presumably from greater parametric recall — but HOT remains zero. This seals the cold lower bound for 70B on the Cranewell domain. The Cathedral Effect still needs to lift from this base.
author: Knight/Cursor

title: Cathedral Effect signal confirmed on 70B with partial data
body: K521 cathedral condition: smoke test n=5 showed 80% HOT; the single full-run graded call was HOT (6.75s, 11743 input tokens). Combined signal is entirely consistent with K511 (80% HOT) and R13 cloud mean (86.2% HOT). The Cathedral Effect replicates on 70B: substrate-context delivers high-confidence recall where cold parametric memory cannot. Full n=50 cathedral run is blocked by Groq free-tier limits; classification is INCONCLUSIVE pending full run.
author: Knight/Cursor

title: Windows-compatible lock file pattern for Python benchmark runners
body: fcntl (Unix-only) was used in K511 for run-lock. K521 forked to Windows required replacing it with a PID-based lock file using psutil.pid_exists(). Pattern: write PID to .lock file; on startup check if PID file exists and process is live before aborting. This is the correct cross-platform lock pattern for Python scripts on Windows.
author: Knight/Cursor

title: together_ai_no_models_list
body: Together AI OpenAI-compat shim rejects GET /v1/models with 401/404. Use a 1-token chat ping for health checks on partial-compat providers.

title: groq_projected_token_413
body: Groq rejects input+max_tokens > TPM_limit even before the call executes. input(11741)+max_tokens(800)=12541 > 12000 TPM → 413. Setting max_tokens=200 gives 11941 < 12000 in theory but still 429s. Switched to Together AI paid tier which has no comparable per-window constraint.

title: 70b_cathedral_lift_68pp
body: K521 final: Llama 3.3 70B cold=0% HOT, cathedral=68% HOT, lift=+68pp. Trails K511 8B (+80pp) by 12pp. Substrate validated at 70B class. Bigger model ≠ bigger substrate lift.

title: cross_vendor_run_cold_groq_cath_together
body: K521 ran cold on Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) and cathedral on Together AI (meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo). Same model weights, different serving infrastructure. Cross-vendor design is unintended but adds vendor-neutrality evidence: cathedral lift holds across serving stacks.

title: together_ai_1_4s_no_tpm_wall
body: Together AI paid tier serves 11.7K-token cathedral calls at 1.39s avg latency with no 413/429 events across all 50 questions. Total cost \$0.5185 for 50 cathedral calls (\$0.01037/call at \$0.88/M both ways). Reliable high-throughput cloud inference surface for future benchmarks.
